_meta.description: Human-verified gold standard. Each field was read by hand from the cited official documentation page (not an LLM verifier). Used to score agent pass-1 vs pass-2 on the fields that can be grounded objectively in docs. auth_methods and api_surface.type are doc-objective; access_tier is a documented judgment call and is scored separately.
_meta.verified_by: manual docs cross-check via direct fetch
_meta.enum_notes: auth_methods in {oauth2, api_key, basic, token, other}; a personal/API token = token, an API key = api_key, HTTP Basic = basic, a developer/non-standard token = other.

apps.quote: The Stripe API uses API keys... authenticates requests using HTTP Basic Auth. Provide your API key as the basic auth username value. Test mode secret keys are free.
create a personal access token, generate a token with a GitHub App, or use OAuth... Basic authentication for specific app endpoints; username/password not supported. GitHub also has a GraphQL API.
Bot tokens (xoxb-), user tokens (xoxp-), app-level tokens (xapp-) obtained via the OAuth flow/OAuth scopes.
Internal connections use a static API token; personal access tokens use a user-scoped token; public connections use the OAuth 2.0 protocol.
API tokens (used with basic auth email/token), OAuth access tokens (Bearer), and legacy basic authentication are supported.
Google Ads API also uses the OAuth 2.0 protocol... In addition to the OAuth 2.0 credentials, you also need a developer token to make API calls (developer token requires application/approval).
OAuth 2.0 is the industry-standard protocol... session tokens and access tokens acquired through token exchange or authorization code grant. Admin API offers REST and GraphQL.
Salesforce APIs use OAuth 2.0 for connected apps; free Developer Edition org is available; REST and GraphQL APIs are offered.
